title: Push: Allow an empty destination
body:
In this case assume it's the same as source. This makes it
compatible with `docker push`.

Thus `buildah push my_registry/my_image` will behave like
`buildah push my_registry/my_image docker://my_registry/my_image`

comments.body: If you like the idea I can go ahead and update docs. My use case is to be able to replace `docker push` with `buildah push`
This looks ok to me.  Do we need similar changes to podman push?

You also need to sign your PR.

git commit -a --amend -s
git push --force
@mheon @baude FYI
Along with docs, a test or two would be good too.
@jwendell We would like to get this into the next buildah release scheduled for tomorrow.  Are you able to continue working on it?
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably ab9547f) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@rhatdan sure.
@rhatdan done, PTAL.
The push tests are breaking, but I'm not sure why.  It doesn't look like it should be due to this change.  Going to try and retest.
bot, retest this please
@TomSweeneyRedHat yeah, they are failing even locally (only the test "push-flags-order-verification")

@jwendell That test expect an error like

buildah push img1 dest1 -q
No options (-q) can be specified after the image or container name

What does your patched version of buildah do?
@rhatdan what I am saying is that even on master that test is failing.

Works on my F28 box?

./tests/test_runner.sh push.bats
++ time bats --tap push.bats
1..4
ok 1 push-flags-order-verification
ok 2 push
ok 3 push with manifest type conversion
ok 4 push with imageid

real	0m14.028s
user	0m3.132s
sys	0m1.648s

@rhatdan you're right! What happened here was that I switched to master branch and re-run the test, and it also failed, because I didn't rebuild buildah binary. So, I assumed master was broken too :/

Anyway, just fixed the error and pushed again.
@rh-atomic-bot r+
